title: Tachycardia documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports clinician assessment of relevant symptoms, pulse, ECG, and ambulatory ECG context without automatically diagnosing atrial fibrillation.

source.title: Atrial fibrillation: diagnosis and management
source.population: Adults with suspected, diagnosed, or previously treated atrial fibrillation or atrial flutter.
source.setting: Primary, outpatient, acute, and specialist cardiovascular care.
source.exclusions: Exact for clinician-led AF detection, risk and cardiac-function assessment, documented care review, and anticoagulation review. Treatment recommendations, named medicines, risk-score conclusions, and doses are not mapped, and UAE pathways require local adaptation.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1-1.1.3 - detection, 12-lead ECG, and ambulatory ECG

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician assessment of relevant symptoms, pulse, ECG, and ambulatory ECG context without automatically diagnosing atrial fibrillation.

assessment.text: Supports symptom history, event frequency, symptom-rhythm correlation, clinical context, and ambulatory ECG review for syncope or palpitations without concluding causation.
assessment.rationale: The HRS consensus supports documenting symptom timing, frequency, associated symptoms, and symptom-rhythm correlation when an ambulatory record is available; it does not classify tachycardia.

source.title: 2017 ISHNE-HRS expert consensus statement on ambulatory ECG and external cardiac monitoring/telemetry
source.population: Adults and selected paediatric patients requiring ambulatory ECG or external cardiac monitoring for symptom-rhythm correlation or defined clinical indications.
source.setting: Outpatient and ambulatory rhythm evaluation, monitor selection, recording, analysis, and clinician result review.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting symptoms, event frequency, symptom-rhythm correlation, monitor type and duration context, recording quality, and clinician review of Holter or ambulatory ECG data. It is old and does not generate a rhythm diagnosis, monitor order, treatment, or referral.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports symptom history, event frequency, symptom-rhythm correlation, clinical context, and ambulatory ECG review for syncope or palpitations without concluding causation.

assessment.text: Supports clinician assessment of relevant symptoms, pulse, ECG, and ambulatory ECG context without automatically diagnosing atrial fibrillation.
assessment.rationale: NG196 supports clinician pulse assessment and an already performed 12-lead or ambulatory ECG when AF is suspected, without treating all tachycardia as AF.
